Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=玉敷神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|一の鳥居}}]] '''玉敷神社'''（たましきじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|加須市|Q851680}}騎西にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。{{jalink|元荒川|Q11388047}}流域に分布する{{jalink|久伊豆神社|Q55523154}}の総本社的存在の神社である。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大己貴命}} === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}3年（{{jalink|703年|Q23500}}）、東山道鎮撫使・多次比真人三宅磨によって創建された。一説には、{{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}6年、{{jalink|武蔵国造}}・{{jalink|兄多毛比命}}の創建ともいう。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では「{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|埼玉郡|Q11427160}} 玉敷神社」と記載され、小社に列格している。 元は現在地の北方の埼玉郡正能村（現：{{jalink|加須市|Q851680}}正能）に鎮座していた。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}2年（{{jalink|1574年|Q6680}}）、{{jalink|上杉謙信|Q311080}}が武蔵に出兵した際に兵火にかかり、{{jalink|社殿}}を焼失した。このとき{{jalink|古文書|Q89298567}}・社宝等も焼失したため、それ以前の{{jalink|歴史|Q309}}については不詳である。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に埼玉郡根古屋村（現：{{jalink|加須市|Q851680}}根古屋）の{{jalink|騎西城|Q11668528}}大手門前に再建された後、{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}4年（{{jalink|1627年|Q6833}}）ごろに現在地に{{jalink|遷座}}した。 江戸時代までは「久伊豆大明神」とも称されており、埼玉郡の総鎮守として尊崇されていた。 === 境内 === ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿（加須市指定文化財） ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社（左端に摂社宮目神社） ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|イチョウ（本殿脇、加須市指定天然記念物） ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|イチョウ（神楽殿脇、加須市指定天然記念物） ファイル:厳島社への石橋 (2023.03.19).jpg|厳島社への石橋 ファイル:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 === 主な祭事 === * 1月1日 ** 元旦祭 ** 歳旦祭 * 2月1日 ** 初春祭・神楽奉奏・だるま市 ** 毎年この日にはだるまが販売され、奉納の舞も見ることができる{{Cite book|和書 |title=さきたま文庫・26 玉敷神社 |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=三松堂印刷株式会社 |page=26}}。 * 2月 節分祭・豆まき・厄除け祈願 * 4月下旬 お馬くぐり ** お馬くぐりとは、戦前では麻疹が流行した際、子供が麻疹にかかり、重症になることを防ぐために2頭の馬の腹下をくぐるものである。戦後になると、麻疹は流行しなくなったためこの行事は見られなくなった。しかし戦後は、子供の健康や成長を願うものとして復活した{{Cite book|和書 |title=さきたま文庫・26 玉敷神社 |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=三松堂印刷株式会社 |pages=30-31}}。 ** 神馬像は{{jalink|1785年|Q7756}}(天明5年)3月吉日の銘があるもの、{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}(明治14年)に奉納されたものの2頭ある{{Cite book|和書 |title=さきたま文庫・26 玉敷神社 |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=三松堂印刷株式会社 |page=30}}。 * 5月5日 春季大祭・神楽奉奏・藤祭り * 6月最終日曜 茅の輪くぐり * 7月15日 夏季祭・神楽奉奏 * 12月1日 例大祭・神楽奉奏 * 1月〜11月の間 お獅子様 * 「おしっさま」と呼ばれることもある、玉敷神社独特のお祓い行事及び神宝である{{Cite book|和書 |title=埼玉県民俗芸能調査報告書 |date=2004-06-17 |year=2004 |publisher=埼玉県立民俗文化センター |page=12}}。 玉敷神社の神宝であるお獅子様は、五穀豊穣、家内安全の祈祷のため各地に貸し出されていて、猿田彦の面とご神宝・剣の３つで１組になっている{{Cite book|和書 |title=玉敷神社 |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=株式会社さきたま出版会 |page=23}}。一般的な祀り方の例として、迎えたお獅子様をそれぞれ地元の神社の前に安置し、お祀りしたあとに行事が村回りをしてお祓いをするものがある。このような信仰を行う地域は主に県の北東部を流れる元荒川流域の市町村に広がり、群馬県の玉村町や板倉町まで及んでいる{{Cite book|和書 |title=玉敷神社 |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=株式会社さきたま出版会 |pages=23-24}}。 祈祷の目的は季節によって異なり、春は災害除けと豊作、秋は無病息災を願って行われる{{Cite book|和書 |title=埼玉県民俗芸能調査報告書　第16集　玉敷神社神楽 |date=2004-06-17 |year=2004 |publisher=埼玉県立民俗文化センター |page=13}}。 * お水取り 毎月1日 7:00-16:00 ** 自然の地下水であり、江戸初期から薬効のある霊水とされている{{Cite web|和書|title=年中行事 |url=http://www.tamashiki.or.jp/gyougi.html |website=玉敷神社のホームページ |access-date=2023-03-19 |language=ja-JP}}。 === 文化財 === === 重要無形民俗文化財（国指定） === * 玉敷神社神楽 - 奉納は2月1日の初春祭、5月5日の春季大祭、7月15日の夏季祭、12月1日の例大祭に行われる{{Cite web|和書|title=年中行事 |url=http://www.tamashiki.or.jp/gyougi.html |website=玉敷神社 |access-date=2023-03-19 |language=ja-JP}}。本殿に向かって左手に位置する神楽殿で奉奏する{{Cite book|和書 |title=埼玉県民俗芸能調査報告書　第16集　玉敷神社神楽 |date=2004-03-25 |publisher=埼玉県立民俗文化センター |page=36}}。正能地区の氏子が父子相伝で代々伝承してきた{{Cite book|和書 |title=さきたま文庫・26玉敷神社 |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=株式会社さきたま出版会 |page=12}}。現在は、保存会を組織して継承している{{Cite book|和書 |title=埼玉県民俗芸能調査報告書　第16集　玉敷神社神楽 |date=2004-03-25 |publisher=埼玉県立民俗文化センター |page=92}}。 {{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（平成20年）3月13日指定{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/00000850 |website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp |access-date=2023-03-19}}。 === 埼玉県指定文化財 === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|玉敷神社のフジ（玉敷公園内）}}]] * 有形文化財 ** 刺繍三十番神像 - 2019年（平成31年）2月22日指定。 * 天然記念物 ** 玉敷神社のフジ - {{jalink|樹齢|Q11543638}}約400年と推定される。境内の東側に隣接する玉敷神社神苑（現在の玉敷公園）に所在する。{{jalink|1933年|Q18726}}（昭和8年）に現在の場所に移植された{{Cite web|和書|date=2013-04-30 |url=http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |title=６種類のフジの花、満開 埼玉・加須でまつり |publisher=朝日新聞（朝日新聞DIGITAL） |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf 『騎西藤まつり』に関する提言書]}}p. 8 - 加須市（騎西地域まちづくり市民会議）、2013年5月28日、2016年5月10日閲覧。。このフジは、戸室の若山家が寄贈したものとされる[https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/shougai/kankou/kisaichiiki/6557.html 加須市公式サイト「玉敷神社の藤（たましきじんじゃのふじ）」]、加須市、2025年10月30日閲覧。。旧・{{jalink|騎西町|Q996453}}のシンボルとして開花時期は見物客で賑わう。{{jalink|2002年|Q1987}}（平成14年）3月22日指定。 === 加須市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉敷神社神楽殿 - 1992年（平成4年）3月16日指定。 ** 玉敷神社文書 - 1992年（平成4年）3月16日指定。 * 有形民俗文化財 ** 玉敷神社算額 - 幾何学問題を額や絵馬に記したものを算額といい、玉敷神社のものには15問記載されている{{Cite book|和書 |title=たまむすび紀行 |date=2021-08|publisher=宗教法人　玉敷神社 |pages=35-36 }}。1980年（昭和55年）9月30日指定。 ** 神楽講の大絵馬 - 1848年（弘化5年）に奉納された幅180cm×縦151cmの絵馬。神社へ{{jalink|献饌}}（けんせん）している、神楽を始める前の様子を描いたもので、当時から色落ちすることなく残っている{{Cite book|和書|title=たまむすび紀行|date=2021-08|publisher=宗教法人 玉敷神社 |pages=33-34}}。1988年（昭和63年）3月31日指定。 * 天然記念物 ** 玉敷神社のいちょう - 1988年（昭和63年）3月31日指定。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|武蔵国の式内社一覧|Q11546334}} * {{jalink|埼玉県の神社一覧|Q11426538}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} House of worship |Location={{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Otomi Takamiko}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} | Founding = (tradition) {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}}3 years ({{jalink|703 years|Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{jalink|December 2nd|Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{jalink|Hizu Shrine|Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3rd year ({{jalink|703|Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}}6, by {{jalink|Musashi Kunizo}} and {{jalink|older brother Tagehi no Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Saitama District|Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1574|Q6680}}), when {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{jalink|shrine building}} was burned down. At this time, {{jalink|ancient documents|Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{jalink|history|Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}4 ({{jalink|1627|Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{jalink|1785|Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{jalink|2008|Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{jalink|age|Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{jalink|1933|Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{jalink|Kisaimachi|Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

House of worship |Location={{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Otomi Takamiko}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} | Founding = (tradition) {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}}3 years ({{jalink|703 years|Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{jalink|December 2nd|Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{jalink|Hizu Shrine|Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3rd year ({{jalink|703|Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}}6, by {{jalink|Musashi Kunizo}} and {{jalink|older brother Tagehi no Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Saitama District|Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1574|Q6680}}), when {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{jalink|shrine building}} was burned down. At this time, {{jalink|ancient documents|Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{jalink|history|Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}4 ({{jalink|1627|Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{jalink|1785|Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{jalink|2008|Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{jalink|age|Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{jalink|1933|Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{jalink|Kisaimachi|Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

'埼玉県加須市騎西552', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Otomi Takamiko}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} | Founding = (tradition) {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}}3 years ({{jalink|703 years|Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{jalink|December 2nd|Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{jalink|Hizu Shrine|Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3rd year ({{jalink|703|Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}}6, by {{jalink|Musashi Kunizo}} and {{jalink|older brother Tagehi no Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Saitama District|Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1574|Q6680}}), when {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{jalink|shrine building}} was burned down. At this time, {{jalink|ancient documents|Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{jalink|history|Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}4 ({{jalink|1627|Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{jalink|1785|Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{jalink|2008|Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{jalink|age|Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{jalink|1933|Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{jalink|Kisaimachi|Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

'埼玉県加須市騎西552', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Otomi Takamiko}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} | Founding = (tradition) {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}}3 years ({{jalink|703 years|Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{jalink|December 2nd|Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{jalink|Hizu Shrine|Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3rd year ({{jalink|703|Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}}6, by {{jalink|Musashi Kunizo}} and {{jalink|older brother Tagehi no Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Saitama District|Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1574|Q6680}}), when {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{jalink|shrine building}} was burned down. At this time, {{jalink|ancient documents|Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{jalink|history|Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}4 ({{jalink|1627|Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{jalink|1785|Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{jalink|2008|Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{jalink|age|Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{jalink|1933|Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{jalink|Kisaimachi|Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

House of worship |Location={{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Otomi Takamiko}} |Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} | Founding = (tradition) {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}}3 years ({{jalink|703 years|Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{jalink|December 2nd|Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}}Kisai. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{jalink|Hizu Shrine|Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3rd year ({{jalink|703|Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}}6, by {{jalink|Musashi Kunizo}} and {{jalink|older brother Tagehi no Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Saitama District|Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1574|Q6680}}), when {{jalink|Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{jalink|shrine building}} was burned down. At this time, {{jalink|ancient documents|Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{jalink|history|Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{jalink|Kazo City|Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{jalink|Kanei|Q1192913}}4 ({{jalink|1627|Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{jalink|1785|Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{jalink|2008|Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{jalink|age|Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{jalink|1933|Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{jalink|Kisaimachi|Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} * {{jalink|List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, '埼玉県加須市騎西552', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otomi Takamiko|1=UNKNOWN}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]
