'埼玉県加須市騎西552', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otomi Takamiko|1=UNKNOWN}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

'埼玉県加須市騎西552', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otomi Takamiko|1=UNKNOWN}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. '埼玉県加須市騎西552', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otomi Takamiko|1=UNKNOWN}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otomi Takamiko|1=UNKNOWN}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho|1=Taihō (era)}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500|1=703}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154|1=Hisaizu-jinja}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho|1=Taihō (era)}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500|1=703}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160|1=Saitama district}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680|1=1574}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567|1=komonjo}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309|1=History}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756|1=1785}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638|1=tree age}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453|1=Kisai, Saitama}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987|1=2002}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538|1=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

category|Category:Tamashiki-jinja}} [[JA:玉敷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572627}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Tamashiki Shrine |Image=[[File:Tamashiki-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|270px]]
House of worship |Location={{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}}Kisai 552 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otomi Takamiko|1=UNKNOWN}} |Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} | Founding = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho|1=Taihō (era)}}3 years ({{ill|lt=703 years|Q23500|WD=Q23500|1=703}}) |Also known as Kuizu Daimyojin |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Regular Festival={{ill|lt=December 2nd|Q2296|WD=Q2296|1=December 2}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=10.8|label=Tamashiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|26.5|N|139|34|10.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Tamashiki-jinja, ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ichino Torii}}]] ``Tamashiki Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}}Kisai. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. This shrine serves as the headquarters of {{ill|lt=Hizu Shrine|Q55523154|WD=Q55523154|1=Hisaizu-jinja}}, which is distributed in the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} basin. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Taiki Takamiko|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho|1=Taihō (era)}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500|1=703}}) by Masato Miyake, Tajihima, who was the envoy to the Tozando Chinpushu. One theory says that it was founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}6, by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Musashi Kunizo|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=older brother Tagehi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as ``{{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Saitama District|Q11427160|WD=Q11427160|1=Saitama district}} Tamashiki Shrine'' and is ranked as a small shrine. It was originally enshrined in Masanori Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}} Masanori), north of the current location. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|WD=Q6680|1=1574}}), when {{ill|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|Q311080|WD=Q311080|1=Uesugi Kenshin}} dispatched troops to Musashi, he was caught in military fire and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine building|1=UNKNOWN}} was burned down. At this time, {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567|1=komonjo}} and shrine treasures were also burnt down, so the {{ill|lt=history|Q309|WD=Q309|1=History}} before that time is unknown. {{jalink|Kisai Castle|Q11668528} in Negoya Village, Saitama District (currently {{ill|lt=Kazo City|Q851680|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}} Negoya) during the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} }After being rebuilt in front of the Otemon Gate, it was moved to its current location around {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}4 ({{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}}).Until the Edo period, it was also called ``Kuizu Daimyojin'' and was revered as the general guardian of Saitama County. === Precincts === File:Tamashiki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Shrine of worship File:Tamashiki-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Cultural Property designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine (Setssha Miyame Shrine on the far left) File:Tamashiki-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-1.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the main shrine, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Tamashiki-jinja, icho-2.jpg|Ginkgo biloba (beside the Kagura hall, a natural monument designated by Kazo City) File:Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine (2023.03.19).jpg|Stone Bridge to Itsukushima Shrine File:Tamashiki-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Main festivals === * January 1st ** New Year's Day Festival ** Saidan Festival *February 1st ** Early spring festival, Kagura performance, Daruma market ** Daruma dolls are sold on this day every year, and you can also watch the votive dance{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=26}}. *February: Setsubun Festival, bean-throwing, prayers to ward off evil spirits * Late April horse ride ** Ouma-kuguri was a practice in which children would pass under the bellies of two horses during a measles outbreak before the war to prevent children from contracting the measles and becoming seriously ill. After the war, this event was no longer observed as measles was no longer prevalent. However, after the war, it was revived as a wish for children's health and growth.{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |pages=30-31}}. ** There are two sacred horse statues, one with the inscription of March auspicious day in {{ill|lt=1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756|1=1785}} (Tenmei 5th year) and one dedicated in {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} (Meiji 14th year){{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko・26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |year=1991 |publisher=Sanshodo Printing Co., Ltd. |page=30}}.* May 5th Spring Festival, Kagura Concert, and Wisteria Festival * Last Sunday of June: Go through the grass ring *July 15th Summer Festival/Kagura Performance * December 1st Annual festival/Kagura performance * From January to November, Shishi-sama* Sometimes referred to as "Oshisama", it is a unique purification event and sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine. The lion, which is a sacred treasure of Tamashiki Shrine, is lent out to various places to pray for good harvests and family safety, and it is made up of a mask of Sarutahiko and a sacred treasure, a sword. |page=23}}. An example of a common method of worship is to enshrine each lion in front of a local shrine, and after the lion is enshrined, the event goes around the village to purify the lion. The area where this type of worship is practiced spreads mainly to the municipalities along the Moto-Arakawa River, which flows through the northeastern part of the prefecture, and extends to the towns of Tamamura and Itakura in Gunma Prefecture. The purpose of the prayer varies depending on the season, and in the spring it is held to ward off disasters and to pray for a good harvest, and in the fall it is held to pray for good health.* Omizutori 1st of every month 7:00-16:00 ** It is natural underground water and has been considered to be a sacred water with medicinal properties since the early Edo period. === Cultural assets === === Important intangible folk cultural property (designated by the country) ===* Tamashiki Shrine Kagura - Dedications are held at the early spring festival on February 1st, the spring festival on May 5th, the summer festival on July 15th, and the annual festival on December 1st. |language=ja-JP}}. It is performed in the Kagura hall located on the left hand side of the main shrine. {{Cite book|Japanese book |title=Sakitama Bunko 26 Tamashiki Shrine |date=1991-04-20 |publisher=Sakitama Publishing Co., Ltd. |page=12}}The shrine parishioners of the Masanori area have passed down the tradition from father to son. Currently, a preservation society has been organized to carry on the tradition. {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}} (Heisei 20) Designated on March 13th |access-date=2023-03-19}}. === Cultural property designated by Saitama Prefecture === [[File:Japanese wisteria, Tamashiki Park.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine (inside Tamashiki Park)}}]] *Tangible cultural property ** Embroidered 30th God Statue - Designated on February 22, 2019 (Heisei 31). * Natural monument** Fuji at Tamashiki Shrine - {{ill|lt=age|Q11543638|WD=Q11543638|1=tree age}} estimated to be about 400 years old. It is located in the Tamashiki Shrine Shinen (currently Tamashiki Park) adjacent to the east side of the shrine grounds. It was transplanted to its current location in {{ill|lt=1933|Q18726|WD=Q18726|1=1933}} (Showa 8). |publisher=Asahi Shimbun (Asahi Shimbun DIGITAL) |page= |accessdate=2016-05-10 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130622004949/http://digital.asahi.com/articles/TKY201304290262.html |archivedate=2013-06-22}}{{PDFLink|[http://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/cont/s101500/files/kisaitiiki.pdf Proposal on the Kisai Wisteria Festival]}}p. 8 - Kazo City (Kisai Area Community Development Citizens' Council), accessed May 28, 2013, May 10, 2016. . This wisteria is said to have been donated by the Wakayama family of Tomuro. . As a symbol of the former {{ill|lt=Kisaimachi|Q996453|WD=Q996453|1=Kisai, Saitama}}, it is crowded with visitors during the blooming season. {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987|1=2002}} Designated on March 22, 2002. === Cultural property designated by Kazo City === *Tangible cultural property ** Tamashiki Shrine Kagura Hall - Designated on March 16, 1992. ** Tamashiki Shrine Document - Designated March 16, 1992 (Heisei 4). *Tangible folk cultural properties ** Tamashiki Shrine Sangaku - Geometry problems written on frames or votive tablets are called Sangaku, and the one at Tamashiki Shrine has 15 questions. Designated on September 30, 1980. ** Large Ema for Kagura-ko - A 180cm wide x 151cm tall votive dedicated in 1848 (5th year of Koka). It depicts the scene before Kagura begins, when offerings are made to a shrine.{{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Tamamusubi Kiko|date=2021-08|publisher=Religious Corporation Tamashiki Shrine |pages=33-34}}. Designated on March 31, 1988. * Natural monument ** Ginkgo of Tamashiki Shrine - Designated on March 31, 1988 (Showa 63). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinai companies in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture|Q11426538|WD=Q11426538|1=List of shrines in Saitama Prefecture}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Tamashiki-jinja}} * [https://www.city.kazo.lg.jp/soshiki/citypromo/film/rokeisyon/20087.html Tamashiki Shrine] - Kazo City Homepage * [http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9065/ Tamashiki Shrine] - Saitama Prefectural Shrine Agency {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamashiki Shinshiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamashiki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]
